# Status

Last update: 2026-03-08
- Latest sync (2026-03-08 roster-unavailability anti-loop FSM alignment pass):
  - Implemented in:
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`
    - `crates/iroha_executor_data_model/src/isi.rs`
  - Changes:
    - aligned the roster-unavailability reducer/runtime path by removing unreachable roster-recovery branches (`CatchUpIsolated`/`Rejoin` + unused events) and keeping catch-up isolation in the dedicated round-liveness FSM only,
    - restored anti-loop escalation semantics: `CandidatesNoop -> EscalateEpoch -> RotateView`, with deterministic target-size shrink per escalation down to a quorum-safe floor (`commit_quorum_from_len(baseline_len)`),
    - canonicalized deterministic elected rosters before comparison/install so order-only differences do not masquerade as membership changes and cause churn,
    - re-enabled temporary-shrink baseline restore wiring by recording `recovery_pending_baseline_restore[height]` when a shrunk roster is installed,
    - prevented telemetry clobbering by removing round-liveness writes to the roster-recovery state status field.
  - Validation commands (current tree):
    - `cargo fmt --all` (ok)
    - `cargo check -p iroha_core --lib` (fails; blocked by pre-existing AccountId parsing API drift in `iroha_core` paths such as `executor.rs`, `queue.rs`, `tx.rs`, and `smartcontracts/isi/asset.rs`)
    - blocker surfaced earlier in the same chain was fixed in `iroha_executor_data_model/src/isi.rs` (`AccountId::from_str` -> `AccountId::parse_encoded(...).into_account_id()`), but additional parse-callsite drift remains outside this tranche.

    - removed legacy no-roster machine paths/types and deleted disabled legacy no-roster tests,
    - enforced deterministic election seed material `chain_id || height(be) || view(be) || latest_committed_hash` and mode-specific candidate sourcing (`NPoS=stake-active`, `permissioned=trusted validators`) with one attempt key per `(height, view, latest_committed_hash)` recovery slot,
    - wired `ViewChangeCause::RosterUnavailable`, added roster FSM status telemetry (state, dwell, detection/election/wait counters), and removed the obsolete `sumeragi.recovery.no_roster_refresh_retry_per_view` config knob across schema/defaults/fixtures/initializers.

    - tagged roster-proof-missing block-sync fallback attempts into the same per-height recovery-bundle context for deterministic diagnostics,
    - cleared the new gate state on mode-flip and roster-change consensus resets to keep behavior finite and deterministic across topology transitions.

- Latest sync (2026-03-07 consensus recovery hardening + focused/full p95 soak reruns):
  - Implemented in:
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`

  - Changes:
    - hardened no-roster invariant handling so `recover_no_roster_only_when_active_set_empty(...)` now escalates fail-closed when deterministic active targets are non-empty (instead of silently skipping recovery),
    - damped sidecar mismatch churn by suppressing repeated post-final-drop recovery actions and throttling mismatch warnings through `allow_roster_sidecar_mismatch_warning(...)`,
    - reduced lock-lag prune thrash by skipping destructive prune when stall-mode reanchor pull is still gated and prior cycles already churned without pull progress,
    - added sidecar-quarantine/no-progress guard to missing-QC idle rotation so same-height rotation is suppressed unless reservation window admission succeeds,
    - extended ingress backpressure resilience with bounded extra retries for `NoEndpointBackpressure`, plus immediate healthy promotion when all endpoints are unhealthy and a probe succeeds,
    - tightened progress safety in `wait_for_target_blocks(...)` with strict-min-height stall detection and strict p95 enforcement alongside quorum p95.
  - Validation commands (current tree):
    - `cargo fmt --all` (ok)
    - `cargo test -p izanami queue_timeout -- --nocapture` (ok)
    - `cargo test -p izanami strict_divergence_ -- --nocapture` (ok)

- Latest sync (2026-03-04 Tranche 7C h=178/179 stall-loop closure, core + ingress / no new public knobs):
  - Implemented in:

  - Changes:
    - hardened frontier-catchup stall hysteresis so transient unresolved/gap blips (including transient `frontier_catchup_target_height(..) -> None`) retain active stall state for `FRONTIER_CATCHUP_STALL_WINDOWS_TO_ACTIVATE` stall windows unless commit height/frontier identity progresses,
    - added deterministic adaptive frontier reanchor stride by no-progress windows (`1/2/4/8` cadence at `<9`, `9..26`, `27..80`, `>=81`) and applied it to frontier-equivalent reasons while preserving deterministic 2-peer cohort + every-3rd all-peer sweep on emitted windows,
    - unified frontier-equivalent reanchor emission via shared canonical gate keyed by `(frontier_height, canonical_height, window_index)` with dependency-progress-at-emit tracking,
    - suppressed same-window `missing_qc` rotation in `force_view_change_if_idle(..)` when contiguous frontier reanchor already emitted and unresolved dependency progress has not advanced, while preserving liveness via existing `try_reserve_missing_qc_height_stall_rotation_window(..)` path,
    - wired committed-edge sidecar mismatch to one bounded per-window conflict bundle under unresolved contiguous frontier: one `highest_qc_committed_conflict` reanchor attempt + one targeted highest-QC missing-block fetch with sidecar observation skip (quarantine semantics unchanged),
    - tightened deferred QC far-ahead replay boundary under frontier stall to `replay_height > local_height_at_entry + 1` (one far-ahead replay per window retained),
    - added internal ingress lag snapshot plumbing (`IngressLagSnapshot`) and lag-aware endpoint exclusion (`quorum_min_height - endpoint_height > IZANAMI_STRICT_HEIGHT_DIVERGENCE_MAX_BLOCKS`) so lagging endpoints are skipped while healthy non-lagging options exist,
    - hardened queue-pressure stickiness with deterministic exponential cooldown by consecutive queue-pressure failures (bounded shift, no new knob) and blocked early reprobe while healthy alternatives exist; forced-probe fallback now avoids dead-end by probing excluded lagging endpoints when normal candidates are exhausted.
  - Test updates:
    - `frontier_catchup_stall_mode_persists_across_transient_unresolved_blips`

- Latest sync (2026-03-02 Tranche 5 single-peer frontier stall escape, safety/liveness neutral):
  - Implemented in:
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`

  - Changes:
    - added internal lock-lag frontier stall tracking state (`LockLagFrontierStallState`) with derived timings only (`ttl = max(recovery_missing_block_height_ttl, 1ms)`, `stall_window = max(lock_lag_range_pull_cooldown_floor, ttl)`), no public config/API changes,
    - entered stall mode after 3 consecutive no-progress windows on the active lock-lag frontier and clear stall mode when the frontier disappears or local commit reaches the frontier,
    - while stalled, throttled `prune_lock_lag_future_consensus_state(...)` to one prune/reanchor cycle per stall window (prune semantics unchanged),
    - for `reason=\"lock_lag_future_prune\"`, added deterministic stalled reanchor targeting from sorted peers: 2-peer rotating cohort per stalled window and all-peer sweep every 3rd stalled window,
    - while stalled, enforced widened range-pull dedup floor (`max(existing cooldown, 2 * recovery_missing_block_height_ttl())`) without changing consensus safety checks, hard-cap logic, or fail-closed escalation rules,
    - added contiguous frontier reanchor behavior for far-future missing-parent fetch attempts (`frontier_gap_realign`) to avoid direct far-future parent chasing,
    - reduced DA commit quorum base window from `block_time + 4 * commit_time` to `block_time + 3 * commit_time` (multiplier semantics unchanged), and aligned shared-host soak profile constants in `izanami` to tighter DA/recovery windows.

- Latest sync (2026-03-01 missing-QC lock-lag bottleneck hardening, safety-preserving):

    - added lock-lag catch-up detection (`lock_lag_catchup_frontier_*`) based on unresolved ancestry between `locked_qc` and incoming/current highest QC,

    - added deterministic lock-lag future-state pruning/reanchor pass (`prune_lock_lag_future_consensus_state`) and wired it into both stalled-pending and idle missing-QC forcing paths,

- Latest sync (2026-02-27 sub-1s localnet plan tranche: fallback-anchor dedup + backlog hysteresis + vote-only fast-path):
  - Implemented internal-only changes with no CLI/public-config/FSM topology changes in:
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs`:
      - unknown-prev dedup identity no longer keys on fallback anchor (`global: (prev_hash, latest_hash)`, `per-peer: (peer, prev_hash, latest_hash)`),

    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`:
      - backlog-aware hysteresis added to `maybe_force_view_change_for_stalled_pending(...)` for non-near-quorum stalls (extended timeout + backlog-progress grace suppression),
      - near-quorum reduced-timeout gate/cause path unchanged (`ViewChangeCause::QuorumTimeout`).
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/qc.rs`:
      - when equivalent missing-block fetch is already in-flight, duplicate defer path now suppresses vote rebroadcast and range-pull escalation churn for that cooldown window.

      - added early known-block vote-only fast-path before roster-selection/validation pipeline.

    - `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs`:

- Offline certificate issuance UX hardening (`crates/iroha_torii/src/routing.rs`, `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_certificates_app_api.rs`, `IrohaSwift`, `java/iroha_android`, `javascript/iroha_js`, `docs/source/sdk/swift/index*.md`): Torii now derives `OfflineWalletCertificate.operator` from the configured offline-issuer keypair and the draft controller domain, so client draft payloads no longer need an `operator` field. `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft.operator` is now optional/ignored server-side for backward compatibility. Swift/Android/JS SDK draft encoders were updated to omit `operator`, while signed-certificate models still require and validate `operator`.

Last update: 2026-03-04
- pk-deploy App Attest capture-quality gating + archive scan (`scripts/offline_topup/{make_pkdeploy_app_attest_settlement_fixture.sh,scan_pkdeploy_app_attest_payloads.sh}`, `fixtures/offline_allowance/README.md`): hardened fixture bootstrapping so auto-generated settlement fixtures now embed/print attestation capture diagnostics (`attestation_capture_quality`, platform/key/assertion/challenge/report lengths), support `--require-real-device` fail-fast mode, and include source file metadata in fixture JSON. Added a new scanner that walks pk-deploy iOS/Android payload archives, decodes Norito receipts via `offline_bundle_decode`, classifies captures as `real_device_candidate|placeholder|...`, and can fail automation when no real captures exist (`--require-real-device`).

Fixture-driven tests now validate this path across all three SDKs so tx-status/body-message behavior stays aligned.

- Offline settlement submit+poll convenience helpers for SDKs (`java/iroha_android/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/OfflineToriiClient.java`, `java/iroha_android/src/test/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/OfflineToriiClientTests.java`, `java/iroha_android/README.md`, `javascript/iroha_js/{src/toriiClient.js,index.d.ts,test/toriiClient.test.js,README.md,dist/toriiClient.js}`, `IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/ToriiClient.swift`, `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests.swift`, `IrohaSwift/README.md`): added first-class `submitSettlementAndWait`/`submitOfflineSettlementAndWait` helpers so clients can submit offline settlements and automatically poll `/v1/pipeline/transactions/status` in one call. Android helper composes `IrohaClient.waitForTransactionStatus(...)`, includes default-options overloads (no explicit `PipelineStatusOptions` required), and preserves typed failure surfaces (`TransactionStatusException`, `TransactionStatusHttpException`); JavaScript helper accepts combined `{ signal + poll options }`, validates option keys, and throws a clear error when settlement responses omit `transaction_hash_hex`; Swift now decodes optional `transaction_hash_hex` on settlement submit responses, adds `waitForTransactionStatus(...)` plus `submitOfflineSettlementAndWait(...)`, surfaces `PipelineStatusError` with rejection reasons, and caches rejected bundle status after pipeline rejection so subsequent `getSettlementStatus(...)` calls return `.rejected(reason:)` immediately.
- Tests: `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `25 passed`), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "submitOfflineSettlementAndWait|submitOfflineSettlement posts transfer and parses response|submitOfflineSettlement accepts build claim overrides and repair flag|submitOfflineSettlement validates build claim override shape|waitForTransactionStatus surfaces rejection reason on failure status"` (ok; `607 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`), `cd javascript/iroha_js && npm run build:dist` (ok).
- Torii replay status/error visibility closure + iOS simulator build/test unblock (`crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs`, `examples/ios/NoritoDemoXcode/NoritoDemoXcode/ContentView.swift`, `examples/ios/NoritoDemoXcode/NoritoDemoXcode.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`): finished wiring replay helper outcomes to transaction pipeline checks so successful replay submissions now assert response `transaction_hash_hex` parity with queued tx hash and immediate `/v1/pipeline/transactions/status` visibility (`kind=Transaction`, `Queued`), while rejected submissions assert absence of queued/status artifacts and require decoded validation/error payload visibility (JSON or Norito fallback). In parallel, fixed simulator iOS breakages by moving misplaced `ConnectViewModel` members (`init`, environment/default persistence helpers, `walletDeepLink`, `permsJson`, `proofJson`) back into type scope, removing an iOS-15-only `textSelection` call from iOS-14 code paths, correcting `NoritoDemoXcodeTests` host binding (`TEST_HOST`), and enabling app debug testability (`ENABLE_TESTABILITY=YES`) so simulator XCTest can import `@testable NoritoDemoXcode`.

New ignored test `offline_settlements_submit_app_attest_real_device_fixture_replay_persists_final_status_and_reason` now closes the prior visibility gap where replay checks stopped at submit-time `Queued` status and did not verify final settlement rejection semantics (including `platform_signature_invalid` when expected by fixture).

- Torii settlement tx-status + validation-error visibility checks (`crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs`): strengthened offline settlement diagnostics by asserting that `transaction_hash_hex` from `/v1/offline/settlements` is immediately queryable via `/v1/pipeline/transactions/status` (`kind=Transaction`, `Queued`), adding unknown-hash pipeline status coverage that decodes Norito `ValidationFail` payloads and verifies query-style variants (`QueryFailed(NotFound|Conversion(_))`) with non-empty validation text, and requiring duplicate-bundle rejections to expose both `x-iroha-reject-code=duplicate_bundle` and decoded conversion-message content containing `duplicate_bundle` (instead of header-only checks).

- Swift/JS tx-status rejection diagnostics hardening (`IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/{ToriiClient.swift,TxBuilder.swift}`, `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/TxBuilderTests.swift`, `IrohaSwift/README.md`, `javascript/iroha_js/{src/toriiClient.js,test/toriiClient.test.js,index.d.ts,README.md,dist/toriiClient.js}`): aligned pipeline failure reporting with the Android pass so terminal rejection paths expose explicit reasons in client errors. Swift `ToriiPipelineTransactionStatus.Status` now decodes optional `rejection_reason`, `PipelineStatusError` now surfaces `rejectionReason` and includes it in localized failure messages, and tx-builder coverage now verifies reason propagation. JavaScript `TransactionStatusError` now derives/stores `rejectionReason` from pipeline payloads (including nested status objects) and includes it in the error message; typings/docs/dist were updated accordingly.

- Android tx-status/error diagnostics hardening (`java/iroha_android/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/{client/OfflineToriiClient.java,offline/OfflineToriiException.java,client/HttpClientTransport.java,client/TransactionStatusException.java,client/TransactionStatusHttpException.java,client/PipelineStatusExtractor.java}`, `java/iroha_android/src/test/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/{OfflineToriiClientTests.java,HttpClientTransportStatusTests.java}`, `java/iroha_android/README.md`): tightened mobile debugging paths so offline settlement and pipeline polling failures now surface actionable status/error context. `OfflineToriiException` now carries optional `statusCode`, `rejectCode`, and response-body preview fields; `OfflineToriiClient` now propagates `x-iroha-reject-code` and richer HTTP/path/body failure messages; `HttpClientTransport.waitForTransactionStatus(...)` now throws typed `TransactionStatusHttpException` for unexpected HTTP statuses (hash/status/reject/body) and propagates parsed rejection reasons (including alias keys and rejected-status content fallback) into `TransactionStatusException` (message + accessor). README now documents the submitŌåÆwait flow with explicit error-field inspection.

This improves failure triage for App Attest fixture debugging by forcing transaction-output and error-payload visibility in the replay harness itself.

Swift now exposes `ToriiOfflineBuildClaim` and typed decode helpers on `ToriiOfflineBuildClaimIssueResponse`; Android now parses/returns typed `OfflineBuildClaim` via `typedBuildClaim()` (with existing raw `buildClaim()` map retained); JS now normalizes/validates response `build_claim` into a typed shape and updates TypeScript declarations accordingly. This prevents malformed payloads from reaching Torii and aligns examples with accepted hash formats.

- Offline settlement SDK parity for build-claim override submission (`IrohaSwift/Sources/IrohaSwift/{ToriiClient,ToriiOfflineRequests}.swift`, `IrohaSwift/Tests/IrohaSwiftTests/{OfflineToriiEncodingTests,ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests}.swift`, `java/iroha_android/src/main/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/{client/OfflineToriiClient.java,offline/OfflineSettlementBuildClaimOverride.java}`, `java/iroha_android/src/test/java/org/hyperledger/iroha/android/client/OfflineToriiClientTests.java`, `java/iroha_android/README.md`, `javascript/iroha_js/src/toriiClient.js`, `javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js`, `javascript/iroha_js/README.md`): Swift `ToriiOfflineSettlementSubmitRequest` now supports `build_claim_overrides[]` + `repair_existing_build_claims` with model-level encoding and transfer convenience initializers; Android `OfflineToriiClient.submitSettlement(...)` now has an overload that accepts typed per-receipt build-claim overrides and repair mode; JavaScript `ToriiClient` now exposes `submitOfflineSettlement(...)` with `buildClaimOverrides`/`repairExistingBuildClaims` support and request/response normalization.
- Tests: `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter OfflineToriiEncodingTests` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ./gradlew :core:test --tests "*OfflineToriiClientTests*"` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "submitOfflineSettlement|issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok; `600 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`). Full `./gradlew test` on this host still reports an unrelated pre-existing harness failure in `TransactionFixtureManifestTests` (`Expected object for schema`).
- Offline settlement build-claim UX gap closure (`crates/iroha_torii/src/{routing,openapi}.rs`, `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs`, `docs/source/offline_allowance*.md`): `/v1/offline/settlements` now supports per-receipt `build_claim_overrides[]` (`tx_id_hex` + optional `app_id`/build/window fields) and `repair_existing_build_claims` for server-side re-issuance. This resolves Android multi-package app-id ambiguity in settlement auto-issue flow, adds explicit rejection paths for unknown/duplicate/invalid override targeting, and keeps strict behavior when issuer is disabled.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `21 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --lib openapi::tests::generated_spec_includes_documented_paths -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `7 passed`).
- Offline certificate issuance UX hardening (`crates/iroha_torii/src/{routing,lib}.rs`, `crates/iroha_torii/tests/{offline_certificates_app_api,offline_app_api}.rs`, `crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/{user,actual,defaults}.rs`, `IrohaSwift`, `java/iroha_android`, `javascript/iroha_js`, `docs/source/{sdk/swift/index*.md,offline_allowance.md}`): Torii now derives `OfflineWalletCertificate.operator` from the configured offline-issuer keypair and the draft controller domain, so client draft payloads no longer need an `operator` field. `OfflineWalletCertificateDraft.operator` remains optional/ignored for backward compatibility. Added `torii.offline_issuer.legacy_operator_private_keys` so Torii can continue issuing/auto-attaching build claims for certificates signed by previous operator keys after key rotation.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api` (ok; `7 passed`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_build_claims_issue_uses_legacy_operator_key_when_primary_rotated -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_fix_operator_gap cargo test -p iroha_config -- --nocapture` (ok), `cd IrohaSwift && swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests` (ok; `15 passed`), `cd java/iroha_android && JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21 ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Android/sdk ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=~/Library/Android/sdk ./gradlew :android:test` (ok), `IROHA_JS_DISABLE_NATIVE=1 node --test javascript/iroha_js/test/toriiClient.test.js --test-name-pattern "issueOfflineCertificate|topUpOfflineAllowance"` (ok; `597 passed`, `0 failed`, `7 skipped`).
- Kura block-index write atomicity hardening (`crates/iroha_core/src/kura.rs`): added fixed-size `BlockIndex` entry encoding and switched block-index entry writes to single 16-byte writes in live write paths (`append_block_batch`, `write_block_index`, and eviction rewrite temp index generation) to prevent transient `blocks.index` misalignment during concurrent reads.
- Added regression coverage for index-entry wire layout stability: `block_index_encoding_is_fixed_little_endian_layout`.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core block_index_encoding_is_fixed_little_endian_layout -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core read_and_write_to_blockchain_index -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests sumeragi_da::sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`, `231.08s`).
- Domain-owner asset-transfer permission regression fix (`crates/iroha_core/src/executor.rs`): restored initial-executor transfer authorization for source-account domain owners (in addition to direct source account signers and explicit transfer permissions) so `domain_owner_asset_permissions` behavior matches domain-owner semantics used elsewhere.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core initial_executor_allows_transfer_asset_by_source_domain_owner -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_core initial_executor_denies_transfer_asset_without_owner_signature -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `cargo test -p integration_tests domain_owner_asset_permissions -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `203.50s`).
- Integration tests unstable-network partition recovery hardening (`integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/unstable_network.rs`): added an always-on bounded recovered submit probe per round (`recovered_submit_window`) even when partitioned submit previously succeeded, and downgraded that immediate recovered submit to warn-and-retry (instead of hard-fail) so the existing supply wait loop can continue periodic resubmits. This addresses the observed flake where `extra_functional::unstable_network::unstable_network_5_peers_1_fault` could stall at supply `4/5` after recovery.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests unstable_network::tests::recovered_submit_window -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests extra_functional::unstable_network::unstable_network_5_peers_1_fault -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`, finished in `667.24s`).
- Integration tests soft-fork supply flake hardening (`integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/unstable_network.rs`): enabled per-round recovered transaction resubmission during forced soft-fork runs even when `n_faulty_peers == 0` (`allow_supply_resubmit` now gates on either faulty peers or `force_soft_fork`). This addresses the observed `extra_functional::unstable_network::soft_fork` failure mode where supply could stall one round behind (`expected 4`, `seen 3`) after a forced fork without partition faults.
- Tests: `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::unstable_network::tests::supply_resubmit_allowed_for_faults_or_soft_fork -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::unstable_network::soft_fork -- --nocapture` (ok, repeated 3x).
- Integration tests/unstable network supply-consistency hardening (`integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/unstable_network.rs`): removed height-only success shortcuts in round/final supply checks so rounds only pass after observing the expected minted asset value, and changed recovery resubmission gating from one-shot to periodic retry while waiting for supply confirmation (duplicate-safe via existing already-accepted handling). This fixes the observed flake where `unstable_network_5_peers_1_fault` could stall at supply `4` while block height advanced.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::unstable_network::tests::resubmit_gate_respects_flags_and_deadline` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::unstable_network::unstable_network_5_peers_1_fault -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`).
- Build acceleration helper script (`scripts/cargo_fast.sh`, `scripts/AGENTS.md`): local wrapper for Cargo that auto-enables `sccache` when available and probes fast-linker support (`mold`/`lld`/`ld.lld` on Linux, `zld`/`ld64.lld`/`lld` on macOS via `-fuse-ld`) before setting `RUSTFLAGS`, with safe fallback to system defaults when unavailable or unsupported. The linker auto-mode probes only discovered linker binaries. Supports `--target-dir`, `--no-sccache`, `--sccache-dir`, `--no-incremental`, `--zero-debug`, `--linker`, and `--print-env` for deterministic local workflows without changing runtime features.
- Host toolchain acceleration enablement (local machine): installed Homebrew `sccache`, `llvm`, and `lld`, then validated wrapper auto-detection (`sccache=yes`, linker=`/opt/homebrew/bin/ld64.lld`) and persistent local cache placement (`SCCACHE_DIR=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/.cache/sccache`).
- Integration-tests compile-path reduction without feature/runtime changes (`integration_tests/build.rs`, `integration_tests/Cargo.toml`, `integration_tests/fixtures/ivm/*.to`): replaced heavyweight build-time Kotodama/IVM sample generation in `integration_tests/build.rs` with deterministic fixture staging from versioned `.to` files, and removed heavy `integration_tests` build-dependencies (`ivm`, `norito`, `iroha_data_model`, `iroha_crypto`, `iroha_primitives`, `iroha_test_samples`) that were only needed by the previous build script. Added missing fixture samples so the staged prebuilt sample set remains complete (`executor_with_custom_parameter`, `mint_rose_trigger`, `create_nft_for_every_user_trigger`, `query_assets_and_save_cursor`, `smart_contract_can_filter_queries`, `trigger_cat_and_mouse`).
- Build-dependency graph cleanup follow-up for unchanged feature/runtime surface (`crates/ivm/Cargo.toml`, `crates/iroha_js_host/Cargo.toml`): removed unused build-dependencies discovered in crates whose `build.rs` uses only `std` and never references those crates (`ivm`: removed `cargo_metadata`; `iroha_js_host`: removed `napi-build`). This trims unnecessary build-script dependency resolution/compilation without altering runtime behavior.
- Integration-tests dev-dependency cleanup for unchanged feature/runtime surface (`integration_tests/Cargo.toml`): removed unused dev-dependencies (`soranet-relay`, `expect-test`, and direct `sorafs_chunker`) from the `integration_tests` crate. `sorafs_chunker` remains present transitively via existing runtime dependencies (`iroha_torii`/`sorafs_car`), so behavior and feature surface are unchanged while direct manifest edges are reduced.
- `irohad` direct dependency cleanup for unchanged feature/runtime surface (`crates/irohad/Cargo.toml`): removed unused direct dependencies (`tokio-util`, `owo-colors`) and unused dev-dependencies (`json5`, `futures`) from `irohad`. These crates were not referenced by `irohad` sources/tests and are no longer direct edges in the `irohad` compile graph.
- `iroha_cli` direct dependency cleanup for unchanged feature/runtime surface (`crates/iroha_cli/Cargo.toml`): removed unused direct dependency `once_cell` from `iroha_cli`. The crate uses `std::sync::LazyLock` and does not reference `once_cell`.
- `iroha_torii` direct dependency cleanup for unchanged feature/runtime surface (`crates/iroha_torii/Cargo.toml`): removed unused direct dependencies `iroha_macro` and `axum-macros` from `iroha_torii`. No source/tests/benches references remained for either crate.
- Dev/test profile throughput tuning follow-up for faster local compile turnaround (`Cargo.toml`): increased dependency codegen parallelism by setting `[profile.dev.package."*"].codegen-units = 256` and `[profile.test.package."*"].codegen-units = 256` (in addition to existing debug-info reductions). This keeps runtime features unchanged and targets faster local compile throughput.

- Compilation speed-up follow-up for unchanged feature surface (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/build-support/Cargo.toml`, `crates/build-support/src/lib.rs`, `crates/{irohad,iroha_cli,iroha_telemetry,iroha_torii}/Cargo.toml`): removed `vergen` from `build-support` and replaced its remaining `VERGEN_CARGO_*` emission with direct Cargo-env emission (`TARGET`, `CARGO_CFG_FEATURE`) while preserving `VERGEN_GIT_SHA` behavior. Also split `build-support` so `norito` is an optional feature (`norito-json`) and set `build-support` build-dependency edges to `default-features = false` for `irohad`/`iroha_cli`/`iroha_telemetry`/`iroha_torii`, avoiding unnecessary tool-only dependency activation in build-script paths. Added `required-features = ["norito-json"]` on `build-support` bins so no-default-feature builds remain valid for build-script usage.
- Validation + timings (post-change):
  - `cargo fmt --all` (ok).
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass8b_build_support cargo test -p build-support` (ok; `13 + 3 + 6` unit tests passed, doc-tests passed).
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass8c_check_irohad cargo check -p irohad` (ok; finished in `2m54s`).
  - Timed `irohad` cold-build passes remained high-variance under this session load (examples: `check --timings` `2m36s` to `2m37s`; `test --no-run --timings` `3m34s` to `3m45s`; `build --timings` `3m57s`), so this tranche is retained for dependency-graph reduction and build-script-path simplification rather than a single stable wall-clock delta claim.
- Compilation speed-up for unchanged feature surface (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/build-support/Cargo.toml`, `crates/build-support/src/lib.rs`): removed `vergen-git2` and replaced git metadata collection with file-based `.git` discovery/ref parsing that still exports `VERGEN_GIT_SHA` and emits deterministic `rerun-if-changed` hints for `.git/HEAD`, `.git/packed-refs`, and the active symbolic ref file. This removes per-build-script git process spawning while keeping IVM/ZK behavior unchanged (no feature/opcode/syscall gating changes).
- Dev/test profile tuning for faster local compile turnaround (same runtime features): set `debug = "line-tables-only"` for `[profile.dev]`/`[profile.test]`; added `[profile.dev.build-override]`/`[profile.test.build-override]` with `debug = 0`, `codegen-units = 256`; enabled `[profile.test] incremental = true`; and added `[profile.test.package."*"] debug = 0` to reduce dependency debug-info cost in test builds.
- Validation + timings (post-change, isolated targets):
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_opt_pass2_build_support cargo test -p build-support` (ok; `11 + 3 + 6` unit tests passed across binaries, doc-tests passed).
  - Dev/check timing runs remained stable (noise-level deltas): `cargo check -p irohad --timings` `2m33s` (`/tmp/iroha_opt_after_check_irohad/...`) vs `2m37s` (`/tmp/iroha_opt_pass2_check_irohad/...`), `cargo build -p irohad --timings` `3m33s` vs `3m34s`, `cargo check --workspace --timings` `3m08s` vs `3m09s`.

- Sandbox serialized-network drop startup-timeout flake hardening (`integration_tests/src/sandbox.rs`): stabilized `sandbox::tests::serialized_network_drop_completes_on_current_thread_runtime` by setting an explicit per-test peer startup timeout override (`with_peer_startup_timeout(Duration::from_secs(480))`). This keeps the test focused on current-thread runtime drop semantics instead of failing on startup timeout under host contention; local verification on this host observed a passing runtime of `862.22s`, confirming the previous 240s floor was insufficient for this scenario.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests serialized_network_drop_completes_on_current_thread_runtime -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`; filtered targets also completed).
- Offline allowance lineage/build-claim follow-up closure (`crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs`, `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_app_api.rs`, `crates/iroha_torii/tests/offline_certificates_app_api.rs`): completed the remaining gap fixes after newest-first lineage and build-claim enforcement by updating register/reissue/renew test fixtures to carry valid lineage metadata (`scope`, `epoch`, `prev_certificate_id_hex`, `min_build_number`), ensuring future-certificate rejection coverage is isolated from lineage checks, and updating Torii offline app/certificate fixtures to include valid lineage drafts plus signed Apple build claims (and iOS metadata bindings) so settlement/certificate API tests match the new validation contract.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core register_allowance_ -- --nocapture` (ok; `11 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p iroha_core submit_transfer_ -- --nocapture` (ok; `8 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_certificates_app_api -- --nocapture` (ok; `5 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p iroha_torii offline_ -- --nocapture` (ok).
- Sumeragi adversarial RBC flake hardening (`integration_tests/tests/sumeragi_adversarial.rs`): stabilized `sumeragi_adversarial_chunk_drop` and `sumeragi_adversarial_locked_qc_gate_rejects_conflicting_proposal` by evaluating delivery from post-wait RBC session snapshots (all sessions for the target height) and committed height, instead of relying on a single early session snapshot that can lag view/session recovery. Also added unit coverage for the new height-wide delivery helper (`delivered_height_check_scans_all_sessions_for_the_height`).
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_adversarial -- --nocapture` (ok; `12 passed`, `0 failed`, `623.31s`).
- Nexus autoscale repeated-cycle strict retry hardening (`integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs`): stabilized `nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet` by adding bounded per-cycle retries (`AUTOSCALE_MULTI_CYCLE_RETRY_LIMIT=2`) with attempt-scaled load (`96 -> 192`) while preserving strict deterministic scale-out/scale-in transition checks for each cycle attempt. This closes the observed cycle-2 strict expansion timeout where the first strict attempt can miss quorum transition signals under localnet contention.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok; `28 passed`), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `224.57s`).
- Nexus cross-dataspace startup retry stability (`crates/iroha_test_network/src/lib.rs`): fixed stale peer-run cleanup on retry by updating `Network::shutdown()` to call `shutdown_if_started()` for every peer, including peers that already flipped `is_running=false` after an early startup failure. This prevents retry panics at `NetworkPeer::start` (`already running`) when a failed peer left a stale `run` handle.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_test_network network_shutdown_clears_stale_peer_runs_even_when_not_marked_running -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::cross_dataspace_localnet::cross_dataspace_atomic_swap_is_all_or_nothing -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`, finished in `296.26s`).
- ISI gas-calibration benchmark role-setup permission regression fix (`crates/iroha_core/benches/isi_gas_calibration.rs`): fixed `isi-gas-cal/GrantAccountRole` warmup panic (`NotPermitted("Can't register role")`) introduced by initial-executor role-registration permission checks. `setup_role_exists` now uses direct ISI execution (`Register::role(...).execute(...)`) in benchmark bootstrap so role creation is deterministic and does not depend on runtime `CanManageRoles` gating. This keeps benchmark intent intact by measuring `GrantAccountRole`/`RevokeAccountRole` execution costs instead of setup authorization.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `cargo bench -p iroha_core --bench isi_gas_calibration GrantAccountRole -- --warm-up-time 0.1 --measurement-time 0.1 --sample-size 10` (ok; no panic), `cargo bench -p iroha_core --bench isi_gas_calibration RevokeAccountRole -- --warm-up-time 0.1 --measurement-time 0.1 --sample-size 10` (ok).
- SORA parliament JIT sortition hardening + IVM VRF epoch-seed utility (`crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/world.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/state.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/governance/draw.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/tests/gov_council_gate.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/tests/gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil.rs`, `crates/ivm_abi/src/syscalls.rs`, `crates/ivm/src/vrf.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/ivm/host.rs`): completed no-council parliament-mode flow by freezing proposal-time multibody roster snapshots (selection epoch/beacon/roster commitment), switching stage quorum checks to proposal-bound epochs, and enabling proposal approvals without persisted council state in parliament mode. Added bounded bond-tier ticket weighting for citizen draws and retained hostile/Sybil regression coverage. Added new ABI syscall `VRF_EPOCH_SEED (0x7E)` with Norito request/response envelopes for smart contracts to read world-snapshot VRF epoch seeds (with optional latest fallback), wired snapshot hydration through all CoreHost setup paths, and added host/unit coverage for found/missing/fallback cases. Synced ABI/docs artifacts (`ivm` syscall docs/table/spec + golden list).
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_jit cargo run -p ivm --bin gen_syscalls_doc -- --write` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_jit CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test -p ivm --test abi_syscall_list_golden --test syscalls_doc_sync -- --nocapture` (ok; 2 passed), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_jit CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test -p iroha_core --lib vrf_epoch_seed_syscall_ -- --nocapture` (ok; 2 passed), and `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_jit CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 cargo test -p iroha_core --test gov_council_gate --test gov_pipeline_sla --test gov_propose_validation --test gov_enact_deploy --test gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil -- --nocapture` (ok; 20 passed).
- Sumeragi DA Kura-eviction test stabilization (`integration_tests/tests/sumeragi_da.rs`): fixed `sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store` flakiness by replacing per-iteration network-wide height waiting (`network.ensure_blocks_with`) with a timeout-bounded wait on the inspected peer (`peer.once_block(expected_height)`), which matches the scenario assertion scope (rehydration via the selected peer's Kura/DA store) and avoids unrelated lagging peers causing false timeouts.
- Tests: `cargo fmt --all` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_da sumeragi_da_kura_eviction_rehydrates_from_da_store -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test sumeragi_da sumeragi_da_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `4 passed`, `0 failed`, `15 filtered out`).
- Offline allowance/App Attest gap closure (`crates/iroha_core/src/smartcontracts/isi/offline.rs`, `docs/source/offline_allowance.md`): closed follow-up gaps by (1) supporting both App Attest nonce-extension encodings (Apple production DER wrapper + legacy plain OCTET STRING), (2) adding fixed DER fixture decoding coverage plus legacy-format attestation verification coverage, and (3) allowing certificate reprovision/reissue without mandatory balance change by reallocating existing same-controller/same-asset remaining allowance capacity before reserving any uncovered shortfall from on-chain balance.

- Latest sync (2026-02-26 policy-only bottleneck follow-up: near-quorum backlog thresholding + block-sync aggregate-mismatch retry):
  - Implemented the requested internal-only speedups without FSM/public-config changes:
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` + `reschedule.rs` + `propose.rs`: near-quorum reduced-timeout gating now treats transient queue blips as non-blocking (depth-thresholded queue backlog for near-quorum paths) and uses soft-limit RBC backlog checks in forced-VC pending-stall handling.
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/qc.rs`: fast missing-payload recovery in `qc_missing_block_defer(...)` now prioritizes payload/block fetch actions first (targeted missing-block request + range pull + escalation bookkeeping) before vote rebroadcast.
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/block_sync.rs`: `QcValidationError::AggregateMismatch` is now treated as retryable in block-sync QC validation (quarantine/replay path) instead of immediate final drop.
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`: updated near-quorum backlog suppression tests for threshold semantics and added `block_sync_qc_retryable_validation_errors_include_aggregate_mismatch`.

    - no `no block height progress for ...` progress-timeout failure in either rerun;
    - no view-change storm increase (forced VC warnings fell in both runs);

- Latest sync (2026-02-26 consensus stall-time reduction follow-up, policy-only, no FSM/state-topology changes):

    - `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs`: unknown-prev fallback share now has per-peer/per-prev cooldown before repeated large fallback bursts; first-time correctness path unchanged.
    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs`: preserved cached-slot hotfix ordering so observed precommit votes for the same slot continue to prevent forced VC churn.

- Latest sync (2026-02-26 consensus stall-budget removal for 1200s/3600s gates, fixed `--tps 5 --max-inflight 8`):

    - `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/reschedule.rs`: added near-quorum reduced timeout path with `near_quorum_payload_timeout = clamp(2 * rebroadcast_cooldown, 400ms..2000ms)` and enabled it only for payload-missing + near-commit-quorum + no backlog + no active RBC progress.

- Latest sync (2026-02-25 plan implementation finalization, fixed `--tps 5 --max-inflight 8`):
  - Finalized the `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` internal-only patch described in the recovery/throughput plan:
    - `recovery_profile_for(...)` now selects shared-host recovery tuning for stable pilot+soak runs via `is_shared_host_stable_recovery_run(...)` (`nexus`, stable workload, `faulty_peers=0`, `peer_count>=4`, `duration>=1200s`);
    - shared-host load-shape pinning remains soak-only (`is_shared_host_stable_soak(...)`, `duration>=3600s`);
    - submit permit scope is limited to submit RPC/retry in `submit_plan(...)` (prechecks/query + post-failure sync run outside the semaphore);
    - deterministic submit backlog bound is enforced at `max_inflight * IZANAMI_SUBMISSION_BACKLOG_MULTIPLIER`.
  - Kept shared-host baseline constants unchanged in this patch (`IZANAMI_SHARED_HOST_SOAK_TPS_CAP=5.0`, `IZANAMI_SHARED_HOST_SOAK_MAX_INFLIGHT_CAP=8`, `IZANAMI_SHARED_HOST_SOAK_PIPELINE_TIME_MS=900`).

- SORA parliament adversarial/hostile coverage completion (`crates/iroha_core/tests/gov_parliament_adversarial_sybil.rs`, `integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs`): finalized hostile-governance tests for multibody sortition. Core adversarial coverage includes six Sybil/economic-capture regressions (10,000-XOR account economics, deterministic sortition seed stability, budget-splitting advantage, monotonic attacker seat growth, duplicate stage-approval deduplication, and non-member rejection without seat capture). Integration coverage includes blocked hostile takeover without seat capture and captured-hostile malicious deploy/runtime enactment. Captured path now enforces full committee capture semantics with quorum-derived checks (`HOSTILE_PARLIAMENT_QUORUM_BPS`, `hostile_required_approvals()`), and shared helper import noise is suppressed for this target (`#[allow(dead_code)]` on `sora_runtime_governance` module import).

- Nexus STARK cross-dataspace privacy-proof coverage added (`integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_zk_stark_localnet.rs`, `integration_tests/tests/nexus/mod.rs`): added four `zk-stark`-gated localnet scenarios under multi-dataspace Nexus routing that cover (1) successful `VerifyProof` from `ds1` with proof-detail redaction for `ds2` + Nexus observers, (2) successful `VerifyProof` from `ds2` with proof-detail redaction for `ds1` + Nexus observers, (3) preverify/validation rejection on mismatched VK and malformed payload with strict no-record expectations, and (4) runtime cryptographic rejection on tampered STARK payload with persisted `ProofStatus::Rejected`. Leak checks validate absence of binary/hex/base64 proof markers in submitter and observer payloads, and observer coverage includes both opposite restricted dataspaces plus a default Nexus-routed observer (genesis `ivm` account).
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_dataspace_zk_stark_localnet.rs integration_tests/tests/nexus/mod.rs` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_ds_stark cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod --features zk-stark stark_cross_dataspace_verifyproof_validity_without_payload_leak -- --nocapture` (ok; runtime skipped as designed without `TEST_NETWORK_IROHAD_FEATURES=zk-stark`), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_ds_stark cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod --features zk-stark stark_cross_dataspace_verifyproof_rejection_without_payload_leak -- --nocapture` (ok; runtime skipped as designed without `TEST_NETWORK_IROHAD_FEATURES=zk-stark`), live localnet execution `TEST_NETWORK_IROHAD_FEATURES=zk-stark NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_ds_stark_v3 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod --features zk-stark stark_cross_dataspace_verifyproof_validity_ds2_submission_without_payload_leak -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`, `171.19s`), and full-suite reruns `TEST_NETWORK_IROHAD_FEATURES=zk-stark NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_ds_stark_v3 cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod --features zk-stark stark_cross_dataspace_verifyproof_ -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `4 passed`, `0 failed`, `414.32s`, then rerun after explicit submitter redaction assertions: `4 passed`, `0 failed`, `420.29s`; prior reruns were `2 passed`/`2 passed`/`3 passed` as coverage expanded).
- Integration tests/connected peers reconfiguration hardening (`integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/connected_peers.rs`): stabilized `extra_functional::connected_peers::connected_peers_with_f_1_0_1` by making the re-register phase retry submission until roster propagation is observed (`submit_instruction_until_peer_roster`), tolerating transient submit timeout/duplicate responses during retries, and reducing the final status block-height expectation to the post-unregister baseline while keeping roster-based re-registration verification as the commit signal.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/extra_functional/connected_peers.rs` (ok), `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::connected_peers::connected_peers_with_f_1_0_1 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok), `IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PARALLELISM=1 IROHA_TEST_NETWORK_PERMIT_DIR=$(mktemp -d) cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::connected_peers::connected_peers_with_f_2_1_2 -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok).
- Transfer-asset signature-cause error clarity (`crates/iroha_core/src/executor.rs`, `crates/iroha_cli/src/main_shared.rs`): updated the initial-executor rejection message for unauthorized numeric asset transfers to explicitly state the signature requirement (`"Can't transfer asset: source asset owner must sign the transaction"`), and adjusted CLI command-error capture to keep the full error chain (`format!("{:#}", report.current_context())`) so nested rejection causes are visible.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/iroha_core/src/executor.rs crates/iroha_cli/src/main_shared.rs` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib initial_executor_denies_transfer_asset_without_owner_signature -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`), `cargo test -p iroha_cli --bin iroha render_cli_error_includes_command_message -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`).
- Time-trigger integration flake hardening (`integration_tests/tests/triggers/time_trigger.rs`): stabilized `mint_nft_for_every_user_every_1_sec_scenario` against transient consensus lag by (1) driving blocks without per-submit strict height waits, (2) making each driving `SetKeyValue` payload unique (`value_{idx}`) to avoid repeated-identical submission edge cases, and (3) replacing immediate post-drive NFT assertions with bounded polling until all accounts reach the expected count. Added helper coverage for the new threshold predicate (`counts_meet_expected_requires_each_account_to_reach_target`).
- Tests: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod triggers::time_trigger::timeout_tests -- --nocapture` (ok; 5 passed), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha-codex-fix-target cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod triggers::time_trigger::time_trigger_scenarios -- --nocapture` (ok; 1 passed), `cargo fmt --all` (ok).
- SORA runtime-governance helper extraction + overlap-policy regression hardening (`integration_tests/tests/common/sora_runtime_governance.rs`, `integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs`, `integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs`): extracted shared runtime fixture/round orchestration into `tests/common/sora_runtime_governance.rs` and wired both smoke + resilience tests to reuse it (smoke now uses shared governance builder + shared runtime round; resilience uses shared fixture/round paths). Chained runtime scheduling now derives deterministically from on-chain runtime-upgrade state (`max(record.manifest.end_height) + 1`) instead of local-only fixture state. Added regression `sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_rejects_overlapping_runtime_window_proposal` that first enacts a baseline runtime upgrade and then asserts a second overlapping proposal is rejected. Resilience lint hygiene was tightened by removing blanket `#![allow(...)]` from `sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs` and pruning copied local helper scaffolding.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/common/sora_runtime_governance.rs integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience --no-run` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke --no-run` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d KAGAMI_BIN=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/kagami CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience -- --nocapture` (ok; `2 passed`, `0 failed`, `1 ignored`, `486.68s`), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d KAGAMI_BIN=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/kagami CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `0 failed`, `266.01s`). Workspace-wide `cargo fmt --all` remains blocked by a pre-existing missing module path (`crates/iroha_core/tests/test_world.rs`).
- Cross-lane additional hardening gap closure (`crates/iroha/src/nexus.rs`, `crates/iroha/src/client.rs`, `ci/check_nexus_cross_lane_proofs.sh`, `.github/workflows/pr.yml`): added tuple-permutation duplicate-key coverage to ensure dedup only triggers on full `(lane_id, dataspace_id, block_height)` matches (`batch_verification_duplicate_detection_depends_on_full_tuple_permutations`), added malformed JSON decode rejection coverage for status-wire ingestion (`get_sumeragi_status_wire_rejects_malformed_json_payload`, `get_sumeragi_status_wire_rejects_json_payload_missing_required_fields`), and added a dedicated PR CI filter job (`nexus_cross_lane_proofs`) backed by `ci/check_nexus_cross_lane_proofs.sh` running `batch_verification_`, `get_sumeragi_status_wire_rejects_`, and `get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_`.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/iroha/src/nexus.rs crates/iroha/src/client.rs` (ok), `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_cross_lane_gapfill NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 bash ci/check_nexus_cross_lane_proofs.sh` (ok; `batch_verification_`: `6 passed`; `get_sumeragi_status_wire_rejects_`: `3 passed`; `get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_`: `9 passed`).
- SORA runtime-upgrade resilience gap fill (`integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs`): completed the previously incomplete resilience test file by adding a dedicated runtime-governance fixture and two new E2E scenarios: (1) peer-restart fault injection during multi-body approvals (`sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_peer_restart_during_multibody_sortition`) and (2) ignored chained-upgrade soak (`sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_chained_rounds_soak`). The runtime round flow now explicitly waits for `ProposeRuntimeUpgradeProposal` transaction application before referendum polling and mirrors existing governance permission prerequisites (`CanProposeContractDeployment` + `CanEnactGovernance`) so proposal admission is deterministic.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience.rs` (ok), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo check -p integration_tests --test sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience` (ok), and targeted live E2E execution with prebuilt binaries (`NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d KAGAMI_BIN=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/kagami CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_runtime_resilience cargo test -p integration_tests --test sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_peer_restart_during_multibody_sortition -- --nocapture --test-threads=1`) passed (`1 passed`, `0 failed`, `238.81s`).
- Follow-up chained-soak execution (`sora_runtime_upgrade_resilience_chained_rounds_soak`) was validated live with the same prebuilt-binary overrides after hardening round scheduling (`next_runtime_window_start` non-overlap + `RUNTIME_WINDOW_DURATION_BLOCKS=40` to keep activation observable in chained rounds). - Cross-lane gap-closure test completion (`crates/iroha/src/nexus.rs`, `crates/iroha/src/client.rs`): closed the remaining cross-lane test gaps by adding empty-batch acceptance coverage in the SDK helper (`batch_verification_accepts_empty_envelopes`) plus client regression coverage for JSON fallback decoding (`get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_accepts_json_status_payload`), empty envelope responses (`get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_returns_empty_for_empty_envelopes`), and response-order preservation (`get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_preserves_envelope_order`).

- Cross-lane duplicate-key boundary + error-precedence coverage extension (`crates/iroha/src/nexus.rs`, `crates/iroha/src/client.rs`, `integration_tests/tests/nexus/cross_lane.rs`): added explicit non-duplicate acceptance tests where only lane differs at the same dataspace+height (`batch_verification_allows_distinct_lanes_on_same_dataspace_and_height`, `get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_accepts_distinct_lanes_on_same_dataspace_and_height`, `verify_lane_relay_envelopes_allows_distinct_lanes_on_same_dataspace_and_height`) and added error-precedence tests that ensure relay integrity failures are surfaced before duplicate tuple errors (`batch_verification_reports_relay_error_before_duplicate_key_check`, `get_cross_lane_transfer_proofs_reports_invalid_relay_before_duplicate_error`, `verify_lane_relay_envelopes_reports_relay_error_before_duplicate_tuple_check`).

- Testus quorum-first membership-lag hardening + staged soak matrix completion (`integration_tests/tests/testus_public_localnet.rs`, `integrated_test_framework.md`): completed the membership lag policy refactor to `MembershipCycleOutcome { hard_lagged, warning_lagged }`, kept submit/propagation/quorum failures as hard lag, made joiner catch-up stall threshold warning-only, added `membership_churn_warning_cycles` to `SimulationSummary`/JSON, and kept membership backoff hard-lag-only. Additional hardening during soak reruns moved `harness.localnet.stop_joiner()?` earlier in membership leave handling to avoid counting active-connection linger as unregister propagation hard lag. Compile/tests: `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_testus_soak cargo test -p integration_tests --test testus_public_localnet --no-run` (ok) plus focused tests (`stalled_joiner_catchup_marks_warning_without_hard_lag`, `propagation_and_quorum_failures_mark_hard_lag`, `membership_backoff_triggers_only_on_hard_lag`, `simulation_summary_json_includes_membership_warning_cycles`, `joiner_stall_warning_threshold_matches_policy`, membership churn deadline tests) all passed.

- Governance runtime-upgrade referendum e2e coverage (`integration_tests/tests/sora_parliament_lifecycle_smoke.rs`): extended the existing 4-peer SORA parliament lifecycle smoke to include a third proposal path using `ProposeRuntimeUpgradeProposal` with deterministic governance proposal-id computation for runtime manifests, all seven parliament-body approvals (Rules, Agenda, Interest, Review, PolicyJury, Oversight, FMA), plain-ballot voting/finalize/enact flow, and runtime activation verification through `/v1/runtime/upgrades` (`ActivatedAt` at scheduled `start_height`). Added reusable test helpers for runtime proposal-id derivation and runtime-upgrade status polling.

While running the live e2e flow, fixed one compile-time numeric type mismatch (`Mint::asset_numeric(1_u32, ...)`) and aligned runtime-body approvals to council-quorum submissions so the runtime referendum consistently transitions `Proposed -> Open`.
- Nexus autoscale localnet gap-closure pass (determinism + observability + CI) (`integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs`, `scripts/nexus/run_autoscale_soak_matrix.sh`, `.github/workflows/nightly_nexus_autoscale_soak.yml`): tightened strict contraction to require quorum scale-in transitions against the post-expansion transition baseline (cycle-start deltas are now diagnostics only), threaded post-expansion baselines into contraction waits, and updated strict assertions/error context accordingly. Added structured soak artifacts (`autoscale_soak_summary.json`, `autoscale_soak_events.jsonl`) with test-local reporter types (`AutoscaleSoakRunSummary`, `AutoscaleSoakCycleEvent`), deterministic seed wiring (`IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_SEED`), explicit artifact-root override (`IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_ARTIFACT_DIR`), and a test-only forced-fail hook (`IROHA_AUTOSCALE_SOAK_FORCE_FAIL_CYCLE`) so fail paths still flush summary/events. Added a new autoscale soak matrix runner that executes multi-seed ignored soak runs and aggregates directly from summary/events artifacts (CSV + JSON, retry/max-attempt/cycle distributions, expansion/contraction p50/p95). Added nightly + manual CI gate for ignored autoscale soak with artifact upload on both pass/fail.
- Tests: `rustfmt --edition 2024 integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs` (ok), `bash -n scripts/nexus/run_autoscale_soak_matrix.sh` (ok). `cargo fmt --all` currently fails in this workspace due pre-existing missing module path (`crates/iroha_core/tests/test_world.rs`), so workspace-wide fmt could not be completed from this tree. The earlier autoscale validation-blocked note from this update window is now superseded by the completed follow-up validations listed above.
- Nexus autoscale localnet soak resilience hardening (`integration_tests/tests/nexus/autoscale_localnet.rs`): added attempt-based strict-cycle load escalation for soak retries (`96 -> 192 -> 288 -> 384`), restricted cooldown-clearance prechecks to cycle-attempt `1` only (retry attempts no longer fail early on no-load non-empty-height movement), and made expansion top-up pressure load-aware so strict expansion waits maintain sufficient queued load under late-cycle stalls. Added helper/unit tests for retry load profile, cooldown-clearance policy, and scaled top-up behavior.
- Tests: `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_transition cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::tests:: -- --nocapture` (ok; `21 passed`), `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_transition cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_repeats_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --nocapture` (ok; `1 passed`, `228.52s`), and soak validation `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_autoscale_transition cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod nexus::autoscale_localnet::nexus_autoscale_soak_expand_contract_cycles_in_localnet -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok; `1 passed`, `2040.85s`; `cycles completed: 72`; cycle 72 attempt 1 timeout recovered on attempt 2).
- Testus public localnet 3600s multi-seed soak matrix (`integration_tests/tests/testus_public_localnet.rs`): added deterministic seed override `IROHA_TESTUS_SIM_SEED` (default `testus-public-sim`) and executed a 3-seed soak matrix on localnet with artifacts preserved (`IROHA_TESTUS_KEEP_LOCALNET=1`) using seeds `testus-matrix-a|b|c`. Validation command shape: `NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TESTUS_KEEP_LOCALNET=1 IROHA_TESTUS_SIM_SEED=<seed> CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_testus_soak cargo test -p integration_tests --test testus_public_localnet -- --ignored --nocapture --test-threads=1 testus_public_localnet_5tps_churn_stability` (all three runs `ok`, durations `3912.20s`, `3779.54s`, `3789.56s`). Preserved summaries: `target/testus-localnet/testus-simulationaGGTX0/localnet/testus_simulation_summary.json`, `target/testus-localnet/testus-simulationnjNZ7A/localnet/testus_simulation_summary.json`, `target/testus-localnet/testus-simulationxy128G/localnet/testus_simulation_summary.json`. Matrix aggregate: `committed_tps avg/min/max = 5.1786/5.1683/5.1883`, `submitted_tps avg = 5.1830`, `tx_sent_total = 48401`, `tx_submit_errors_total = 0`, `view_changes_end-start total = 0`, `max_height_skew_observed max = 1`, `process_churn_lagged_cycles_total = 0`, `membership_churn_lagged_cycles_total = 7` (non-fatal lag/backoff warnings only).
- Confidential localnet gap-fill completion for shielding/unshielding fault paths (`integration_tests/tests/zk_confidential_localnet.rs`): added end-to-end stress/reliability scenarios `confidential_dual_restart_stress_mid_flow_localnet` and `confidential_combined_peer_downtime_and_timeout_pressure_localnet`, plus malformed-proof rejection coverage `confidential_unshield_rejects_corrupted_proof_bytes_localnet`, `confidential_unshield_rejects_corrupted_vk_bytes_localnet`, and `confidential_unshield_rejects_wrong_statement_hint_localnet`. Added helper/unit coverage for deterministic restart index selection, pressure submitter timeout overrides, and corrupted attachment mutators. Hardened restart flow with idempotent peer stop (`shutdown_if_started`) to avoid panic when a test intentionally restarts an already-stopped peer. Stabilized balance assertions with quorum-based polling (`wait_for_numeric_balance_quorum`) for policy-sensitive flows. Also aligned ZkNative policy coverage with current runtime behavior by replacing `confidential_zknative_transparent_transfer_after_mint_localnet` with `confidential_zknative_transparent_transfer_after_mint_rejected_localnet` (transparent transfer denied, source balance unchanged). Validation: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/iroha_target_zk_conf_gapfill2 NORITO_SKIP_BINDINGS_SYNC=1 IROHA_TEST_SKIP_BUILD=1 TEST_NETWORK_BIN_IROHAD=/tmp/iroha_target_zk_conf_gapfill/iroha-test-network/release/iroha3d KAGAMI_BIN=/Users/takemiyamakoto/dev/iroha/target/debug/kagami cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet confidential_zknative_transparent_transfer_after_mint_rejected_localnet -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok, `1 passed`, `20.91s`), `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet confidential_unshield_rejected_when_disabled -- --nocapture --test-threads=1` (ok, `1 passed`, `17.91s`), and full-file confirmation `... cargo test -p integration_tests --test zk_confidential_localnet -- --test-threads=1` (ok, `34 passed`, `0 failed`, `544.14s`).
- Nexus matrix runner gap-closure hardening (`scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh`): closed the remaining five reliability gaps by (1) adding explicit missing-value guards for all value-taking flags (`Missing value for --<flag>`), (2) fixing timeout attribution to mark `timed_out=1` only when the wrapper timeout marker is emitted (non-timeout exit `124` now remains `timed_out=0`), (3) guaranteeing summary emission on fail-fast exits via `trap finalize_outputs EXIT`, (4) switching timeout execution to process-group-aware termination (`SIGTERM` then `SIGKILL`) to prevent orphan child processes, and (5) tightening `--prefer-test-binary` matching to exact/anchored test-id resolution (avoids substring false positives). Validation: `bash -n scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh` (ok); missing-value checks `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh --cross-runs` and `scripts/nexus/run_cross_runtime_matrix.sh --cross-runs --runtime-runs 1` (both fail fast with `Missing value for --cross-runs`); non-timeout `124` check with fake binary target (`--runtime-timeout-s 0`) produced `/tmp/iroha_matrix_non_timeout_124/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` row `1,124,0,...`; wrapper-timeout check (`--runtime-timeout-s 1`) produced `/tmp/iroha_matrix_wrapper_timeout/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` row `1,124,1,...`; fail-fast summary check without `--continue-on-failure` produced summary artifacts at `/tmp/iroha_matrix_failfast_summary/{matrix_summary.txt,matrix_summary.json}` with partial rows preserved; process-tree cleanup check using spawned child process yielded `child_gone` for recorded pid after timeout; exact-match selection check with `mod-similar` + `mod-correct` selected `mod-correct` and produced `/tmp/iroha_matrix_exact_match/runtime_registration_matrix.csv` row `1,42,0,...`.

- Latest sync (2026-02-25 pilot/acceptance gate prep with fixed `--tps 5 --max-inflight 8`):
  - Root-cause split now tracked explicitly: 1200s pilot failures are recovery-churn dominated (strict/quorum split + fallback-anchor loops), while 3600s under-target runs are submit-path throughput limited (not consensus deadlock).
  - `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` updates:
    - added `is_shared_host_stable_recovery_run(...)` (`nexus`, stable profile, `faulty_peers=0`, `peer_count>=4`, `duration>=1200s`) and switched `recovery_profile_for(...)` to this selector so pilot-scale stable runs use shared-host recovery tuning;
    - kept load-shape pinning (`tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`, progress-timeout floor, pipeline-time floor) gated only by `is_shared_host_stable_soak(...)` (`duration>=3600s`);
    - narrowed semaphore scope: load supervisor no longer acquires submit permits before plan generation/prechecks; `submit_plan(...)` now acquires permit only immediately before submit RPC/retry and releases it right after submit result;
    - added deterministic submit backlog cap `IZANAMI_SUBMISSION_BACKLOG_MULTIPLIER=8` and enforced queue bound `submissions.len() < max_inflight * multiplier` before admitting new submit tasks.
  - Added/updated regression coverage in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs`:
    - `shared_host_recovery_profile_applies_to_stable_pilot_runs`;
    - `shared_host_recovery_profile_does_not_apply_below_pilot_duration`;
    - `submission_permit_scope_does_not_block_precheck_phase`;
    - `wait_for_submission_capacity_enforces_backlog_bound`.
  - Validation:
    - `cargo fmt --all` (ok)
    - `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_stable_soak_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok; 4 passed)
    - `cargo test -p izanami shared_host_recovery_profile_ -- --nocapture` (ok; 2 passed)

- Latest sync (2026-02-25 lagging-peer recovery storm suppression + inflight A/B lock):
  - Root-cause statement: the blocker is lagging-peer recovery storm churn (fallback-anchor + missing-QC/proposal-missing loops), not ingress saturation.
  - Implemented deterministic suppression/cleanup hardening in `crates/iroha_core/src/block_sync.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/propose.rs`, and `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`:
    - unknown-prev fallback serving now suppresses repeated responses for the same requester/frontier unless local canonical state advances enough to provide new content;
    - proposal assembly now tracks one active highest-QC-missing defer marker per `(height, view, highest_qc_hash)` and triggers at most one reacquire action per key until state advancement/cleanup.
  - Shared-host load-shape baseline is now pinned for stable-soak runs in `crates/izanami/src/chaos.rs` (`tps=5`, `max_inflight=8`), with new regression `shared_host_stable_soak_profile_pins_canonical_load_shape`.

- Integration test startup flake mitigation for address-canonicalisation (`integration_tests/src/sandbox.rs`): `start_network_async_or_skip`/`start_network_blocking_or_skip` now perform one bounded retry (`2` total attempts, `1s` delay) when `network.start_all()` fails with retryable peer-bootstrap snapshot errors (`expected peers to start within timeout` / `peer startup failed; startup snapshot`), reducing transient peer-start stalls that were failing `account_transactions_query_supports_address_format` before assertions executed. Added `is_retryable_network_startup_error` classifier coverage (`startup_retry_classifier_matches_timeout_snapshot_errors`, `startup_retry_classifier_ignores_non_startup_errors`).

Validation: `swift test --filter ToriiOfflineEndpointsTests --filter OfflineWalletTopUpTests` (ok; 20 passed), repeated same command x5 (ok each run; no flakes), `swift test --filter testGetSettlementStatusReturnsRejectedFromSubmitRejectCode` (ok; 1 passed, explicit `offline_reason::commitment_mismatch` handling), `swift test --filter Offline` (ok; 151 passed, 7 skipped), repeated same command x3 (ok each run), `swift test` (ok; 722 passed, 18 skipped), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api` (ok; 9 passed), `cargo test -p iroha_torii --test offline_app_api offline_settlements_submit_persists_rejected_record_for_offline_error` (ok; 1 passed), repeated same settlement-rejection command x3 (ok each run), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test offline_allowance_security` (ok; 3 passed), repeated same command x2 (ok both runs; ~217.24s and ~141.12s), `cargo test -p integration_tests --test mod extra_functional::offline_peers::genesis_block_is_committed_with_some_offline_peers -- --nocapture` (ok; 1 passed, ~201.59s), and per-target Torii offline integration sweeps (`offline_certificates_app_api` 5/5, `offline_bundle_proof_status` 2/2, `offline_transfer_detail` 4/4, `offline_transfer_proof` 2/2, `offline_revocations` 2/2, `offline_receipts` 2/2).
- Offline allowance reclaim flake fix (`integration_tests/tests/offline_allowance_security.rs`): `register_topup_expire_then_reclaim_restores_controller_balance` now builds/signs the certificate only after the test network is ready, preventing startup permit waits from consuming certificate TTL before registration. Increased the test certificate expiry window from `1_500ms` to `15_000ms` to keep register-time validation deterministic under slower CI/network scheduling.

- Follow-up hardening for deterministic missing-QC recovery (2026-02-24): `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs` now forces `MissingBlockFetchMode::AggressiveTopology` for highest-QC missing-block reacquire while sidecar quarantine is active; `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop.rs` stale-height pruning now always clears linked validation inflight/superseded state and RBC sessions by `(hash,height)` even if the pending block entry is already absent; and new debug assertions codify the sparse/backoff + stale-prune invariants.

- Landed the deterministic missing-QC recovery FSM tranche across Sumeragi (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop{.rs,/block_sync.rs,/commit.rs,/mode.rs,/proposals.rs,/tests.rs}`, `crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/status.rs`) and config plumbing (`crates/iroha_config/src/parameters/{defaults.rs,user.rs,actual.rs}`, `crates/iroha_config/tests/fixtures.rs`): sparse `BlockSyncUpdate` recovery now uses canonical `plan_missing_block_fetch_with_mode(...)`, `requested_missing_block` becomes effective in the same event transition, stale missing requests are pruned on head advance, deferred/pending queues are bounded with deterministic eviction, and stale missing-QC rounds are converted to cleanup instead of repeated view-change churn.
- Added deterministic recovery knobs in `sumeragi.recovery`: `missing_request_stale_height_margin` (default `16`), `pending_block_sync_cap` (default `256`), `pending_proposal_cap` (default `128`), and `missing_fetch_aggressive_after_attempts` (default `2`), with validation coverage in `iroha_config`.
- Added/expanded consensus telemetry counters: `missing_request_pruned_stale_height`, `pending_queue_evictions_total`, and `missing_qc_trigger_suppressed_stale`.
- Added deterministic regressions for the no-hang FSM path: same-event sparse request registration, backoff suppression without duplicate fetch, stale-height pruning with linked-state cleanup, bounded deferred block-sync eviction priority/tie-break behavior, and stale missing-QC trigger suppression (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/tests.rs`).
- Validation (this tranche): `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib block_sync_update_records_missing_request_when_sparse_signatures_fail_quorum -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib plan_missing_block_fetch_respects_backoff_window -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib apply_commit_outcome_updates_view_change_install -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib prune_stale_missing_requests_clears_linked_state_and_records_counter -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib deferred_block_sync_queue_cap_prefers_evidence_and_newer_views -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib deferred_block_sync_queue_cap_tiebreaks_by_hash -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_core --lib force_view_change_if_idle_suppresses_stale_missing_qc_round -- --nocapture` (ok), `cargo test -p iroha_config --test fixtures -- --nocapture` (ok), `bash scripts/formal/install_apalache.sh 0.52.2` (ok), `bash ci/check_sumeragi_formal.sh` (ok, `[formal] sumeragi Apalache checks passed`).

- Canonical Izanami 3600s soak rerun (same parameters, direct binary path) was executed twice on 2026-02-23 and still failed to complete the 3600s horizon. Inference from artifacts: run terminated on the same no-progress/liveness failure family before reaching target horizon.

- Closed a concrete missing-block liveness gap in consensus fallback routing (`crates/iroha_core/src/sumeragi/main_loop/votes.rs`): when commit/QC-derived rosters are empty, missing-block fetch now deterministically falls back to `trusted_topology()` instead of bailing out with no targets. This applies to both highest-QC fetch recovery and unresolved-roster vote-driven fetch seeding.
